name: IBM OpenPages GRC Platform API
what_it_does: IBM OpenPages GRC Platform REST API for enterprise governance, risk, and compliance management with AI-powered automation. Enables AI agents to manage risk and compliance workflow automation with Watson AI assistance, handle regulatory change management and compliance framework mapping, access enterprise audit management and finding lifecycle tracking, retrieve third-party and operational risk assessment data, manage SOX, GDPR, and regulatory compliance program automation, handle policy management and attestation campaign workflows, access AI-powered risk scoring and predictive analytics, retrieve financial control and IT risk data integration, manage model risk and AI governance framework compliance, and integrate GRC data with IBM Watson, Cognos analytics, and enterprise ERP platforms.
use_cases: Automating enterprise risk workflows with AI from risk management agents
Managing regulatory compliance mapping from compliance automation agents
Tracking audit findings from enterprise audit agents
Integrating GRC data with IBM Watson from AI governance agents
not_for: Lightweight compliance tracking without enterprise IBM platform investment
SMB risk tools without IBM enterprise ecosystem requirements
Developer tools without GRC segregation of duties requirements
best_when: A large enterprise using IBM OpenPages wants AI agents to automate GRC workflows, leverage Watson AI for risk scoring, manage regulatory compliance mapping, track audit findings, and integrate with IBM ecosystem.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: Automated risk acceptance and control attestation must preserve segregation of duties — automation cannot both perform and approve the same control. Watson AI risk scores should augment, not replace, human risk judgment in material risk decisions.

auth.notes: IBM OpenPages uses IBM Cloud IAM OAuth 2.0 or basic auth depending on deployment (Cloud vs on-premises). Role-based access control with module-level permissions. Documentation on IBM Docs. No native webhooks — scheduled extract for data integration. IBM Cognos Analytics integration for reporting. Watson AI integration for risk insights.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — IBM enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual enterprise subscription; IBM Cloud SaaS or on-premises deployment
pricing.notes: Armonk, New York. IBM Corporation. NYSE: IBM. OpenPages acquired by IBM (2010, $50M). IBM OpenPages 8.x with Watson AI integration. Part of IBM Security portfolio. Strong financial services, banking, and insurance vertical. SOX automation capability. Model Risk Management (MRM) for financial services AI governance. Competes with MetricStream and RSA Archer for enterprise GRC.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits depend on deployment type; Cloud has IBM standard rate management

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Automated GRC actions must preserve segregation of duties — automation identity should not approve its own actions
Cloud vs on-premises deployment — auth model differs (IBM Cloud IAM vs local auth); verify deployment type before implementing auth
Watson AI integration — AI-powered risk features require Watson API configuration separate from OpenPages REST
Complex GRC object model — OpenPages uses a highly configurable object hierarchy; each deployment's schema differs by configuration
No native webhooks — implement scheduled polling or IBM Integration Bus connectors for event-driven integration
IBM Docs documentation — comprehensive docs on IBM Docs portal; requires navigation through version-specific content
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise GRC with AI. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. IBM Cloud IAM/OAuth2. US/EU. Risk, compliance, and audit data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise GRC. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. IBM Cloud IAM. US/EU. Risk, compliance, and audit program data.
